sections.webBody: Use FleetShare in your browser: register your company, invite drivers, and manage reservations from the dashboard. No install required on desktop.
sections.mobileBody: Install the FleetShare Android client on company phones for reservations, digital glovebox, and notifications on the go.
sections.pricesBody: Plans depend on fleet size and hosting (cloud or self‑hosted). Contact us for a quote tailored to your organisation.
sections.contactBody: Questions, demos, or technical support — we are here to help.

homeExplore.title: Where to next?
homeExplore.subtitle: Open the web app, read about the Android client, check pricing and hosting options, or send us a message.
homeExplore.webHint: Create your company account and use the dashboard in the browser.
homeExplore.mobileHint: See what the Android app offers drivers and fleet admins.
homeExplore.pricesHint: Interested in hosting FleetShare on your own company server?
homeExplore.contactHint: Demos, quotes, or technical questions — we reply by email.

contactForm.pageSubtitle: Send us a request and we will get back to you by email.
contactForm.success: Thank you — your message was sent.
contactForm.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
contactForm.errorConfig: The contact form is not configured on this server (email inbox).

mobileApp.title: FleetShare for Android
mobileApp.subtitle: Native client for your company fleet
mobileApp.intro: The app uses the same secure session and REST API as the web dashboard. Your IT team provides the server URL; features below match the current Android build.
mobileApp.bullet1: Sign in with email and password; session cookies are stored securely on the device.
mobileApp.bullet2: Dashboard with fleet overview: cars, active reservations, and team members.
mobileApp.bullet3: Reserve vehicles with scheduling, pickup codes, and release codes when supported by your server.
mobileApp.bullet4: Digital glovebox during an active reservation: ITP, RCA, and vignette documents on your phone.
mobileApp.bullet5: Offline cache for reservations and cars so key data stays visible when the network drops.
mobileApp.bullet6: Home screen widget for your next or current booking (where the launcher supports widgets).
mobileApp.bullet7: Push notifications for reservation reminders when Firebase Cloud Messaging is configured.
mobileApp.bullet8: Admin tools where your role allows: manage cars and users, invites, history, company settings, statistics, audit logs, maintenance, incidents, reports, and fleet calendar.
mobileApp.bullet9: Driving licence upload and approval flow aligned with the web platform before booking.

pricesPage.title: Prices & company server
pricesPage.subtitle: Hosting and quotes
pricesPage.body: If you are interested in running FleetShare on a self-hosted company server — your infrastructure, your data — please contact support for sizing, deployment options, and a tailored quote.
pricesPage.supportLead: Our team can walk you through Docker-based deployment, security, and ongoing maintenance.

landing.heroSub: Reserve vehicles, validate licences automatically, and track fleet costs — all in one platform built for modern teams.
landing.selfHostedDesc: FleetShare can be installed on your company’s own servers or cloud account so the platform stays independent — you control hosting, domains, and who accesses your fleet data.

features.fleetDesc: Track every vehicle in real time — availability, mileage, and maintenance status.
features.teamDesc: Employees reserve cars instantly or in advance with built-in conflict detection.
features.aiDesc: Automatically validate driving licences with Gemini AI before approving reservations.
features.costDesc: Track fuel consumption, CO₂ emissions and costs with detailed PDF reports.

branding.fleetSmarterSub: Reserve vehicles, track usage, and keep your team moving — all from one place.

download.unavailableBody: Installers and public app packages are paused on this site for now. Please check back later or use the contact form if you need access for your organisation.
download.pageSubtitle: Use online, deploy on your company’s server, or install the Android app
download.intro: Use the web app in your browser, run the full stack on your own machine with the installer below, or install the Android app on company devices.
download.androidBody: Install the FleetShare Android client on your phone or tablet. Download the APK from this site, then open it to install (you may need to allow installs from unknown sources).
download.apkFileHint: Android package (.apk), not a Windows .exe. Hosts may ship the file as public/downloads/fleetshare.apk or via NEXT_PUBLIC_ANDROID_DOWNLOAD_URL.
download.apkInstallSteps: On your phone: after downloading, open the file from the notification or Downloads app. If prompted, allow installs from your browser (or Files). The APK must be signed (debug or release) or Android will refuse to install.
download.webBody: No install required: register or sign in from any modern browser. Works best when your company deploys FleetShare on a URL your team trusts.
download.aiValidatorIntro: Optional add-on: FleetShare can send licence photos to a small Gemini-based service on port 8080. Use it alongside the company server installers above.
download.aiValidatorDownloadsHint: Requires Docker and git on the machine. Files are served from this site — no extra hosting.
download.aiValidatorRunHint: Linux / macOS: chmod +x the .sh file, then run it. Optional parent folder as first argument (e.g. ..). Details and environment variables are in the .txt file.
download.aiValidatorAfter2: Run the .sh or .ps1 installer. You can place the validator folder next to your FleetShare project if you pass .. or -Parent ...
download.aiValidatorAfter3: Add your Gemini API key in the validator project’s .env file (see the README inside that folder after install).
download.aiValidatorAfter4: FleetShare in Docker usually talks to this service at host.docker.internal:8080, or you can set a company Gemini key in Admin instead.
download.fullServerBody: For Ubuntu, a VM, or Windows Server: download the installer and run it on the machine that will host FleetShare. It prepares the full Docker stack (database, web app, HTTPS front-end), detects this server’s address, sets the public URL and port (default 3000), and starts everything for you.
download.fullServerDownloadsHint: You need Docker and git on the server. After downloading, run the installer from any folder.
download.fullServerRunHint: Linux / macOS: chmod +x the .sh file, then run it. Optional: pass a parent folder as the first argument (e.g. ..). You can set host, port, or source location via environment variables documented in the .txt file.
download.fullServerAfter1: A local project folder with the full FleetShare server edition, ready to run in Docker.
download.fullServerAfter2: The database, application (default port 3000), and Caddy for HTTPS (see the project’s deploy notes, often port 8443 locally).
download.fullServerAfter3: Other devices on your network can open the app at http://THIS_SERVER_IP:3000 (or the port you chose).
download.fullServerAfter4: For a public domain and TLS, add your reverse proxy or follow the Caddy setup in the deployed project, then update the public URL in configuration and rebuild the app container if required.

login.subtitle: Sign in to access your fleet dashboard.

register.title: Create your account
register.subtitle: Join FleetShare and manage your company cars.
register.name: Full name
register.passwordRule: Password must be at least 8 characters.
register.heroSub: Create your account and get your team set up in minutes.
register.cardBody: Secure reservations, licence checks, and fleet analytics in one place.
register.quickSetupBody: Register → Create your company → Invite your team → Start reserving cars

admin.addUserBlurb: Creates the account immediately (Local database). If you leave password empty, a default temporary password is used. We email the user with a sign-in link; on first login they must choose a new password before using the app. Configure Resend (RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM) for mail.

stats.title: Statistics & Reports
stats.rangeHint: Charts and trip totals use completed reservations whose end time falls in the range. Top users count reservations that started in the range.
stats.co2Hint: 2.31 kg/L Benzine, 2.68 kg/L Diesel, Electric uses grid factor.
stats.unitPricesSub: Set in Company settings (admin). Used for cost estimates below.
stats.co2Empty: No CO₂ data in the last 30 days
stats.efficiencySub: Ranked from most efficient (lowest consumption) to least. L/100km for fuel cars, kWh/100km for Electric/Hybrid.
stats.rangeEmpty: No EV/Hybrid cars with battery data
stats.serviceSub: Oil change >10,000 km since last service (fuel/hybrid); battery check for electric.
stats.costLeaderSub: Cars ranked by total estimated fuel/electricity cost (highest = least efficient).
stats.costLeaderSubPeriod: Completed trips in {period}. Cars ranked by estimated fuel/electricity cost in that window.
stats.fuelTrendNoPrice: Set fuel or electricity prices in Company settings to see estimates
stats.fuelTrendEmpty: No fuel expenditure data in the last 30 days

settings.hybridHint: Liquid fuel part; falls back to Benzine/Diesel if empty.
settings.legacyPrice: Legacy: single "Price per Liter" below (used if per-type prices not set).
settings.defaultKmHint: Allowed km per reservation. Users must give a reason if they exceed this.
settings.defaultConsumptionHint: Used when a car has no consumption set (e.g. 7.5).

Hi! I'm your **FleetStream AI Assistant**.

I can help you with:

Ask me anything!

legal.disclaimer: This document is provided as general information only and does not constitute legal advice. FleetShare operators should have policies reviewed by qualified counsel for their jurisdiction.

privacy.s1Body: FleetShare (“we”, “us”, “our”) provides a company car sharing platform. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect personal information when you use our website, APIs, and related services.
privacy.s2Body: We may process: account details (such as name, email, and role), company and fleet data you enter, reservation and vehicle usage information, driving licence data you or your organisation submit for verification, technical logs (IP address, browser type, timestamps), and communications you send to support. Exact categories depend on how your organisation configures the service.
privacy.s3Body: We use data to provide and improve the service: authentication, reservations, notifications, reporting, security, fraud prevention, and compliance with applicable law. Licence-related data may be processed by automated or AI-assisted checks where your organisation enables those features.
privacy.s4Body: Processing is typically based on contract (providing the service to your organisation), legitimate interests (security and product improvement), or consent where required. We may use subprocessors (e.g. hosting, email, analytics) under appropriate agreements. We do not sell your personal information.
privacy.s5Body: We retain information for as long as needed to operate the service and meet legal obligations. We apply technical and organisational measures designed to protect data; no method of transmission or storage is completely secure.
privacy.s6Body: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to certain processing, and to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. Contact your organisation’s administrator or us using the details your deployment provides.
privacy.s7Body: If servers or subprocessors are located outside your country, we rely on appropriate safeguards where required by law (such as standard contractual clauses).
privacy.s8Body: We may update this policy from time to time. We will post the revised version with a new “Last updated” date. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance where permitted by law.

terms.s1Body: By accessing or using FleetShare, you agree to these Terms. If you use the service on behalf of a company, you represent that you have authority to bind that organisation. If you do not agree, do not use the service.
terms.s2Body: FleetShare is a software platform for managing shared company vehicles, reservations, users, and related workflows. Features may change; we may modify, suspend, or discontinue parts of the service with reasonable notice where practicable.
terms.s3Body: You must provide accurate registration information and keep credentials confidential. You are responsible for activity under your account. Notify your administrator or us promptly of any unauthorised use.
terms.s4Body: You must not misuse the service: no unlawful activity, no attempt to breach security, no interference with other users, and no scraping or excessive automated access except as permitted in writing. Organisations are responsible for their users’ compliance.
terms.s5Body: You retain rights to data you submit. You grant us a licence to host, process, and display that data solely to provide the service. We may generate aggregated or anonymised analytics that do not identify individuals.
terms.s6Body: The service is provided “as is” to the maximum extent permitted by law. We do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation. Vehicle availability, licence validity, and on-road compliance remain your organisation’s responsibility.
terms.s7Body: To the extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or loss of profits, data, or goodwill. Our aggregate liability for claims relating to the service is limited to the amounts you paid us in the twelve months before the claim (or, if none, a nominal amount).
terms.s8Body: These Terms are governed by the laws applicable to the contracting entity operating your FleetShare deployment, unless a separate written agreement states otherwise. Courts in that jurisdiction have exclusive venue where permitted.

support.s1Body: For login issues, driving licence uploads, reservations, or company access, contact your organisation’s FleetShare administrator first. They can reset access, approve licences, and manage fleet settings.
support.s2Body: If something does not load, try refreshing the page or signing out and back in. Clear your browser cache if problems persist. Administrators can check service status, environment configuration, and integration settings for your deployment.
support.s3Body: Questions about how personal data is handled are covered in our Privacy Policy. Your organisation is often the data controller for employee and fleet data; we process it to provide the service as described there.
support.s4Body: Ask your internal IT or fleet team for the right contact for this FleetShare instance (for example a shared inbox or ticketing system). Generic product documentation may be available from your organisation.

cookies.s1Body: This page describes how FleetShare uses cookies and similar storage on your device. We keep tracking to a minimum and focus on what is needed to run the app securely.
cookies.s2Body: We use HTTP-only session cookies (or equivalent) so you can stay signed in and so our servers can protect your account. These are strictly necessary for the service and cannot be turned off while you use the dashboard.
cookies.s3Body: Your language and currency choices may be saved in your browser’s local storage so the site remembers them between visits. This is not used for advertising and does not track you across other websites.
cookies.s4Body: This deployment does not use third-party advertising cookies. Optional analytics, if enabled by your organisation’s hosting or configuration, would be described separately; you can ask your administrator what is active for your environment.
cookies.s5Body: You can block or delete cookies and site data in your browser settings. Note that blocking essential session cookies may prevent sign-in or cause the app to stop working until you allow them again for this site.
cookies.s6Body: For details on personal data, see our Privacy Policy. If you need organisation-specific practices (retention, subprocessors, DPA), contact your FleetShare administrator.

userSecurityForm.intro: When enabled, signing in sends a 6-digit code to your email after you enter the correct password.
userSecurityForm.enabledOk: Email sign-in code is now on.
userSecurityForm.disabledOk: Email sign-in code is now off.
userSecurityForm.bookingEmailsHint: Receive confirmation and updates when reservations are created, cancelled, extended, or when an admin decides on extra km.
userSecurityForm.calendarFeedIntro: Add this URL to Outlook or Google Calendar to sync your upcoming FleetShare bookings (private link — do not share).
userSecurityForm.calendarFeedHelpAria: How to add this link in Google Calendar
userSecurityForm.calendarFeedGoogleStep1: Open Google Calendar in a browser: calendar.google.com
userSecurityForm.calendarFeedGoogleStep2: On the left, under Other calendars, click the + button.
userSecurityForm.calendarFeedGoogleStep4: Paste the FleetShare link (use Copy link above), then click Add calendar.
userSecurityForm.calendarFeedGoogleStep5: Events may take a few hours to appear; Google refreshes subscribed calendars periodically.
